purpose: Second human-eval batch, applying the rules distilled from batch #1: no warhorses, no figure-hints in the stem, natural (non-gerrymandered) filters, clean inversions, lean into surprising answers and bottom-end records. NOT filed to the library.
rules_applied: No chestnuts (largest landlocked / longest coastline / smallest country / city-on-two-continents).
No stem leaks the answer's discriminating figure.
Filters are ones a player naturally reasons with, not carve-outs.
Several bottom-end / surprising-answer items; each on its own dimension.

candidates.q: Which country has a greater share of its land covered by forest than any other?
candidates.note: Beats Gabon (~91%) and Guyana (~87%). Surprising — not a country most would name. Finalists Suriname, Gabon, Guyana, Bhutan.

candidates.q: Of all the countries where people drive on the left, which has the most people?
candidates.note: Natural filter; reasoner lists left-driving (ex-British) countries. Finalists India, Indonesia(no, right), Japan, Pakistan, Bangladesh.

candidates.q: The Equator runs through more than a dozen countries. Which of them is the largest by land?
candidates.note: Finalists Brazil, DR Congo, Indonesia, Colombia, Kenya.

candidates.q: Only two countries are 'doubly landlocked' — you must cross two borders to reach any sea. Which of the two is larger?
candidates.note: The other is Liechtenstein. Like the Lesotho item: concept is the delight, 'larger' is a clean tiebreak.

candidates.q: Which is the most populous country whose capital is not also its largest city?
candidates.note: Shanghai ~28M > Beijing ~22M. India excluded (Delhi is both). Finalists China, USA, Brazil, Pakistan, Australia.

candidates.q: Which country has fewer people for each square mile of land than any other?
candidates.note: Greenland isn't sovereign. Finalists Mongolia, Namibia, Australia, Iceland.

candidates.q: Only two countries in Africa were never formally colonized by a European power. Which of the two is larger?
candidates.note: The other is Liberia. Mirrors the 'fun filtered record' that rated well in batch 1.

candidates.q: Which country has more UNESCO World Heritage sites than any other?
candidates.note: China is one behind at 60 — a genuine coin-flip bet. Finalists Italy, China, Germany, France, Spain. Flag: count shifts yearly.

candidates.q: Which is the only country whose own territory sits in all four hemispheres at once — north and south, east and west?
candidates.note: Sits where the Equator meets the date line. France/US/UK qualify only via overseas territories. Reasoning: must cross equator AND 180° → a spread-out Pacific nation.

candidates.q: People in which country drink more coffee per person than anywhere else?
candidates.note: Nordic sweep; finalists Finland, Norway, Iceland, Netherlands, Sweden. Natural per-capita, no carve-out.

candidates.q: Which country has more people who speak Spanish as their first language than any other — more even than Spain itself?
candidates.note: Finalists Mexico, Colombia, Argentina, USA. Spain (~47M) is far down. Border 'more than Spain' is a boundary to reason against, not the answer's figure.

candidates.q: Which is the smallest country on the African mainland?
candidates.note: Seychelles is smaller but an island nation — flag the mainland qualifier. Finalists Gambia, Eswatini, Djibouti, Rwanda. Re-sliced bottom-end record.
